one_line_thesis: Gyrodyne, LLC is winding itself down and selling its last two real estate properties (Flowerfield on Long Island and Cortlandt Manor in Westchester County). The company's own disclosed liquidation-basis net asset estimate is $11.79 per share as of March 31, 2026, versus a market price near $5.80, a roughly 51% discount. If that estimate is realized by the company's own stated 2028 target dissolution date, the annualized return is about 33% per year; even a stressed bear case (extra year of delay, weaker price on the one signed land-sale contract) still returns a positive 13% to 14% per year.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Gyrodyne is winding itself down. It owns two properties on Long Island and in Westchester County, is pursuing zoning approvals to raise their sale value, and intends to dissolve once everything is sold, likely by the end of 2028. The company discloses its own estimate of what shareholders should receive: about $11.79 per share as of March 31, 2026, versus a market price near $5.80. If that estimate holds and the timeline holds, buying today and collecting that amount works out to about 33.4% per year. We treat this as an asset story, not an earnings story: the return depends on approvals, a...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

investment_committee.strongest_dissent: The roughly 51% discount may be fair compensation for a re-estimatable management NAV, pending Article 78 litigation, a B2K contract already marked at its high end, twice-extended liquidation timing, possible dilutive financing with no per-share bridge modeled, and daily volume too thin to arbitrage the gap.
unresolved_items: Outcome of the Article 78 appeal and current Town of Smithtown site-plan status for the B2K parcel, both still pending as of the frozen 10-Q filed 2026-05-13.

The expected outcome of the October 2026 Rialto mortgage maturity, including current loan-to-value and debt-service coverage against the documented extension thresholds and any post-freeze extension, refinance, or default record.

Whether Article 78 has been decided and whether Smithtown granted final site-plan approval on the B2K parcel, because the base real-estate NRV already uses the $28.74M high-end B2K assumption and the research loop did not close these catalyst statuses.

decision.next_action: Close critical evidence gaps before freezing a decision-grade packet.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: Component changes are classified from observable differences in quantity, comparable method, probability, timing, capital, evidence, and schedule scope.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Real estate held for sale at estimated NRV

components.cross_check: Range must reconcile to primary filings and realization path before IC approval.

economic_value.accounting_reference: ASC 205-30 liquidation-basis 10-Q Q1 2026 statement of net assets in liquidation ($11.79/sh).

economic_claim.description: One Gyrodyne, LLC common share claim on net assets in liquidation after debt, estimated liquidation costs, and entitlement contingencies.
economic_claim.unit_source: 10-Q Q1 2026 cover page, shares outstanding as of 2026-05-13
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Assets at estimated NRV less loans, other liabilities, and estimated liquidation/operating costs equals disclosed net assets in liquidation. B2K option is embedded in real-estate NRV.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Real estate held for sale at estimated NRV
valuation_proof.evidence: 10-Q Q1 2026 liquidation-basis statement of net assets; proof base 24.5486/sh via net_asset_value@1.0 (GYRO/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260513_rpt20260331_acc0001437749_26_016537.htm).
valuation_proof.adjustment: Entitlement and sale timing sit in the range. Embedded option documented in component_valuation with treatment=embedded.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key real_estate_nrv; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Forced sale or failed entitlement marks real estate NRV below the low case.

valuation_proof.evidence: 10-Q Q1 2026 liquidation-basis statement of net assets; proof base 1.872/sh via net_asset_value@1.0 (GYRO/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260513_rpt20260331_acc0001437749_26_016537.htm).
valuation_proof.adjustment: Burn through 2028 is partly in the cost accrual.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key cash_and_other_assets; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Cash burn through 2028 consumes more than the low-case residual.

valuation_proof.evidence: 10-Q Q1 2026 liquidation-basis statement of net assets; proof base -4.9062/sh via net_asset_value@1.0 (GYRO/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260513_rpt20260331_acc0001437749_26_016537.htm).
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key loans_payable; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Refinancing or default increases the claim above the low case.

valuation_proof.evidence: 10-Q Q1 2026 liquidation-basis statement of net assets; proof base -7.5978/sh via net_asset_value@1.0 (GYRO/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260513_rpt20260331_acc0001437749_26_016537.htm).
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key estimated_liquidation_costs; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Further re-estimation (as in FY2025 NAV drop $13.91→$11.76) pushes costs beyond the low case.

valuation_proof.evidence: 10-Q Q1 2026 liquidation-basis statement of net assets; proof base -2.1293/sh via net_asset_value@1.0 (GYRO/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260513_rpt20260331_acc0001437749_26_016537.htm).
valuation_proof.adjustment: Plug to reconcile to $11.79 net assets.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key other_liabilities; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Unmodeled claims exceed the low case.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Real estate held for sale at estimated NRV
valuation_proof.evidence: B2K agreement $24.0–$28.74M on ~49 acres; base NRV already uses $28.74M. Embedded so not added twice; high case is premium to current NRV if entitlements exceed the plan.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Entitlement and sale timing sit in the range. Embedded option documented in component_valuation with treatment=embedded.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key real_estate_nrv; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Article 78 or Smithtown denial collapses the B2K path and forces a re-mark below current NRV.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Target liquidation completion 2028-12-31; Article 78 can extend into 2029 in bear.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Base assumes Smithtown site plan path supporting the \.74M gross already in NRV.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Seller credit \ and entitlement costs are inside the liquidation cost accrual.

timeline.label: Long-term debt was not reported in the prior period and is now 10,868,531.0 (USD thousands).
Q1 2026 10-Q: net assets in liquidation $25,924,002 ($11.79/sh), up slightly from $25,858,997 ($11.76/sh) at 12/31/2025 on a favorable expense variance
Star Agreement signed: Star Equity Fund LP withdraws board nomination notice; board reduced from five to four directors; standstill through 12/31/2026 (or 2027 if conditions met)
B2K Agreement signed: sale of ~49 acre Flowerfield parcel to B2K Smithtown LLC for $24.0M-$28.74M, contingent on subdivision and site-plan approval
Star Equity Fund LP (claimed ~7.1% ownership) delivers notice of intent to nominate two board candidates
Rights offering closed: approximately $4.4 million net proceeds, extending the company's ability to fund operations through the liquidation period
Loeb Cooperation Agreement with Leap Tide Capital Management: Jan Loeb appointed to the board, standstill and voting commitments

Article 78 Proceeding commenced by the Incorporated Village of Head of the Harbor challenging the Town of Smithtown's approval of the Flowerfield subdivision application; appeal fully briefed as of July 2025 and still pending
Town of Smithtown Planning Board grants preliminary approval to divide Flowerfield into eight lots
Gyrodyne files subdivision applications for both Cortlandt Manor and Flowerfield, beginning the current multi-year entitlement process
Stipulation of Settlement in a putative class action: Gyrodyne agrees to sell remaining properties only at or above December 2014 appraised values

industry.structure: GYRO is not really participating in an industry in the ordinary sense; it is a wind-down vehicle holding two New York State real estate parcels (an industrial park in Suffolk County and medical office buildings in Westchester County) that it is entitling and marketing for sale through commercial brokers (JLL). Comparable situations are other single-purpose real estate liquidation trusts and small entitlement plays, not operating REITs.
industry.share_shift: Not applicable; there is no market share to gain or lose. The relevant dynamic is local zoning and land-use approval, which is idiosyncratic to Suffolk County (Smithtown) and Westchester County (Cortlandt) governments.
industry.trend: Long Island and Westchester County suburban office and industrial land continues to see slow, litigation-prone entitlement processes; medical office demand (Cortlandt Manor's designated use) has generally been more resilient than traditional office demand nationally.
peers: Other small real estate liquidation / wind-down vehicles (no direct public comparable found)
KEWL (different asset class, similar micro-cap illiquidity / hidden-asset dynamic)

top_claims.claim: Long-term debt was not reported in the prior period and is now 10,868,531.0 (USD thousands).

Gyrodyne is winding itself down. It owns two properties on Long Island and in Westchester County, is pursuing zoning approvals to raise their sale value, and intends to dissolve once everything is sold, likely by the end of 2028. The company discloses its own estimate of what shareholders should receive: about $11.79 p
We expect about **33.4%** per year at $5.80, assuming the company's own disclosed liquidation-basis net asset estimate of $11.79 per share is realized by the December 31, 2028 target date.

*Primary risk: The Article 78 court appeal on the Flowerfield subdivision is not yet resolved (10-K FY2025 Item 3, Legal Proceedings), and the company itself says it does not expect to sell Flowerfield until that appeal is decided. An adverse ruling could force a redesign of the subdivision plan, delay the B2K sale bey

*Primary risk: The Article 78 court appeal on the Flowerfield subdivision is not yet resolved (10-K FY2025 Item 3, Legal Proceedings), and the company itself says it does not expect to sell Flowerfield until that appeal is decided. An adverse ruling could force a redesign of the subdivision plan, delay the B2K sale bey

Financing risk: management states in the 10-K FY2025 MD&A that it may need additional capital to fund operations through the 2028 target date; a dilutive raise would reduce per-share value even if the underlying real estate sells as planned.
